name: Spicy, Tomato-Based Vegetable Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat enough vegetable oil in a large pot.
2. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
3. Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute.
4. Stir in 1/2 hot chili pepper, reserving some for garnish if desired.
5. Add the diced carrots, turnips, and parsnips to the pot. Cook until they start to soften.
6. Incorporate the quartered mushrooms and continue cooking until all vegetables are tender.
7. Pour in enough chicken stock to cover the vegetables by about an inch.
8. Stir in 1 can of tomato paste and season with oregano, basil, and any other herbs or spices you prefer.
9. Bring the stew to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and let it cook until flavors meld together (about 30-40 minutes).
10. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed before serving.
11. Serve the stew over couscous or your preferred grain.
Note: This recipe freezes well, so consider making large batches for future meals.
